systemStatus.headline: Tue Jul 14, 7 AM run -- the new 7 AM slot works: Monday Jul 13 Toast numbers are IN, and so is today's Sling schedule. Two things need you today: (1) Monday's labor ran 35.8% of net -- about double normal -- and (2) Toast payroll is DUE TODAY.
systemStatus.detail:
Tue Jul 14 7 AM run -- all five sources captured (Toast + Sling by email, Google Ads + Yelp by live Chrome scrape). Moving this job from 4 AM to 7 AM closed the structural gap the last several runs kept flagging.

TOAST (Mon Jul 13): net $2,750.05 / gross $2,819.25 / 120 guests / 91 orders / avg guest $22.92. LABOR 35.8% OF NET ($984.52) -- roughly double the 18-19% this location normally runs, and the number to look at first. Sling had 8 staff / 31.58 hrs scheduled against $2,750 of sales; the roster was not cut to match a slow Monday. Caveat before you act: Rafael Patricio was auto-clocked-out AGAIN (his Sling shift reads the malformed '5:59A - 5:59A'), and an unclosed shift inflates labor cost -- so check his timecard before assuming all 35.8% is real. No SDLW/4-week comparison yet: those live in the location-level email that lands ~7:40 AM, after this run.

SLING (Tue Jul 14, today): 10 employees / 11 shifts / 47.82 hrs / 0 uncovered / 0 OT / 0 off -- a 51% jump in hours over Monday. Sarah Palmer doubles (Barista then Runner). Isael Salvador Narciso's Cook shift shows the same broken '4:34A - 4:34A' pattern -- that Sling entry bug is now hitting a second employee and is worth fixing at the source, since it is what drives the auto-clock-out and the inflated labor line.

GOOGLE ADS (live, Jul 13): $58.41 / 7,159 impr / 140 clicks / CTR 1.96% / CPC $0.42 / 0 conversions. BHC Coffee | Search | P1 Launch is bleeding: $27.09 for 9 clicks = $3.01 CPC, 12.5x the PMax campaign's $0.24. It took 46% of the day's spend for 6% of the clicks, and its optimization score has slid to 38.9% (46.9% yesterday, 50.8% on Jul 10). Conversions still read 0 across the account because the 'Reservation Form Submission' tag was never verified on the website -- until that is fixed, no campaign can be judged on ROI.

YELP (live): last 30d 28.8k ad impressions, 398 clicks, 524 page visits, 121 leads. Billing period Jul 1-13: $181.45, CPC $0.99, CPL $2.93 (improving). Yelp is the more efficient channel right now. Bonus budget remaining still not surfaced.

REVIEWS: no new ones overnight, but Sunday's two 5-star reviews are still unanswered (David Makosch also added 4 photos). Backlog 22.
TRIPADVISOR: no ad report. YELP INQUIRIES: 0 actionable.

PAYROLL -- ACTION TODAY: Toast Payroll (Jun 28 - Jul 11) is DUE TODAY Jul 14, pay date Jul 17, 2 action items, needs funding.

NOTE ON TIMING: a ~8:00 AM run would additionally capture Toast's richer LOCATION-level email (SDLW, 4-week average, void $, dayparts, rooms), which still arrives ~7:40 AM -- just after this run.

toast.source: GROUP-level Daily Performance Summary (The Brewhouse Santa Barbara Group, Mon Jul 13), email received Tue Jul 14 6:22 AM PT -- parsed on the Tue Jul 14 7 AM run. The LOCATION-level email (which carries SDLW + 4-week-average comparisons, voids $, dayparts and rooms) lands ~7:40 AM PT and was not yet available.
toast.note: MON JUL 13: Net $2,750.05 / Gross $2,819.25 / 120 guests / 91 orders / avg guest $22.92 / avg order $30.22 / discounts 2.5% ($70.48 derived) / 2 void items / refunds $0 / 16 takeout orders ($145.50) / 66 card payments / 0 open orders. *** LABOR 35.8% OF NET ($984.52 derived) -- roughly DOUBLE the 18-19% this location normally runs, and the single most important number on this dashboard today. Monday is the weakest sales day, but the schedule was not cut to match: Sling had 8 staff / 31.58 hrs against $2,750 of net sales. Rafael Patricio was auto-clocked-out again (same as prior runs) -- an unclosed shift inflates labor, so part of the 35.8% may be a punch-out error rather than true cost. Worth checking the timecard before treating it as real. No SDLW or 4-week comparison available: the group email does not carry them and the location email had not arrived.

sling.note: TODAY (Tue Jul 14), delivered 7:00 AM PT: 10 employees / 11 shifts / 47.82 scheduled hours / 0 uncovered / 0 OT / 0 off. That is a big step up from Mon Jul 13 (8 staff / 31.58 hrs) -- +51% scheduled hours. Sarah Palmer works a double (Barista 6:30A-12:30P, then Runner 12:30P-2:30P). Isael Salvador Narciso's Cook shift reads 4:34A - 4:34A, the same malformed 24-hour pattern Rafael Patricio's shifts have been showing -- likely a Sling entry error, not a real shift.

googleAds.note: LIVE Chrome scrape Tue Jul 14 ~7:25 AM PT. Yesterday (Jul 13): $58.41 / 7,159 impr / 140 clicks / 0 conv. *** BHC Coffee | Search | P1 Launch is the problem line: 174 impressions and 9 clicks cost $27.09 -- an avg CPC of $3.01, which is 12.5x the $0.24 the PMax campaign pays. It burned 46% of the day's spend for 6% of the clicks, and its optimization score has now fallen to 38.9% (was 46.9% on Jul 13, 50.8% on Jul 10 -- a steady week-long slide). PMax-2 remains Eligible (Limited): limited by budget AND all asset groups limited by policy. World Cup HQ 2026 and Oldest SB Brewery on Tap still paused. 2 drafts in progress.

billingPeriod.note: Billing period Jul 1-13, 2026: ad spend $181.45, Avg CPC $0.99, Avg CPL $2.93.

yelpAds.note: LIVE Chrome scrape Tue Jul 14 ~7:30 AM PT. Last 30d: 28.8k ad impressions (72% of 40k total), 398 ad clicks, 524 page visits from ads (57% of 914), 121 leads from ads (58% of 210). Billing period Jul 1-13: $181.45 spend, $0.99 CPC, $2.93 CPL -- only $11.91 added on Jul 13 (vs $17.43 on Jul 12), the lightest day of the period. CPL keeps improving: $2.93, down from $2.97 yesterday and $3.31 last week. Yelp is delivering leads at roughly one-third the cost per click of the BHC Coffee search campaign. Bonus budget remaining is still not surfaced anywhere on the ads dashboard. The 'Finish setting up your ad -- 1 thing remaining' prompt is still showing.

David Makosch also uploaded 4 photos to the Business Profile overnight -- an engaged customer worth a reply. Backlog remains 22 unanswered across Google + TripAdvisor.

toastPrevDay.note: SUN JUL 12 FINAL (location-level): Net $6,860.86 / Gross $7,050.86 / 259 guests / 159 orders / labor $1,273.50 (18.6%) / discounts $190.00 / voids $162.50. vs SDLW (Sun Jul 5) -23.5%; vs 4-Sunday avg ($7,425.90) -7.6%. Jul 5 was the holiday-weekend Sunday, so the -23.5% is a soft comp; the -7.6% vs the 4-Sunday average is the honest read.

toastPending.reason: Nothing pending. Moving this run from 4 AM to 7 AM PT fixed the structural gap: the Toast GROUP daily summary (6:22 AM PT) is now captured same-morning. The richer LOCATION-level email (~7:40 AM PT, adds SDLW / 4-week avg / voids $ / dayparts / rooms) still lands after this run -- an ~8:00 AM run would capture that too.

payroll.note: ACTION TODAY: Toast Payroll for Default Payroll - BIWEEKLY (Jun 28 - Jul 11) is DUE TODAY, Jul 14, with pay date Jul 17. Toast lists 2 action items and 2 insights, and payroll must be funded. Missing the due date pushes the pay date.
